Items.Key: os command injection in admin command execution|os command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|606|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|0606: std::string cmd = command.substr(5); 0608: system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated user requesting system commands via 'admin exec <command>' can inject arbitrary shell commands, leading to remote code execution (rce) with the privileges of the application.|authenticated input provided to the admin command 'exec <command>'. e.g., 'exec whoami > /tmp/pwned'.|do not pass externally derived strings directly to system(). for executing operational commands, use dedicated os libraries (like fork/exec family functions on linux) which allow explicit passing of arguments, preventing shell interpretation.

truthAudit.Summary: The audit confirms that both detected instances of OS Command Injection are present in the source code. The usage of external/untrusted strings combined with system() or concatenated system commands directly demonstrates the vulnerability.
